text:
**First Information Report (FIR)**

**Complainant:** Dr. Anand Rao, 58, Retired Professor, Resident of 123, Malleswaram, Bengaluru.

**Date of Incident:** 15th October 2023

**Time of Incident:** Approximately 11:30 AM

**Place of Incident:** 123, Malleswaram, Bengaluru

**Narrative:**

On 15th October 2023, around 11:30 AM, I, Dr. Anand Rao, received a video call on my mobile phone from an individual who identified himself as a CBI officer. The caller claimed that my Aadhaar number was linked to a money laundering case and that immediate action was required to resolve the issue. 

The caller, using a professional demeanor and official jargon, coerced me into transferring Rs 42,00,000 to the account number 9842017633250001 (HDFC Bank, IFSC HDFC0004217). The transaction was made through UPI using the identifier raghu.ctrl@ybl. 

The caller provided a device IMEI number 867537042195016, which he claimed was used to track the case. I was intimidated into believing that failure to comply would result in my arrest and further legal consequences. 

Upon realizing the fraudulent nature of the call after hanging up, I checked my bank account and found the amount had been debited. I immediately contacted my bank and reported the scam. 

I am lodging this complaint to bring this cybercrime to the attention of the authorities and seek appropriate action against the perpetrators. I request a thorough investigation into this matter and the recovery of the transferred amount.

**Complainant Signature:**

[Dr. Anand Rao]  
Date: 16th October 2023
